personas.name: Cost-Sensitive Mental Health Seeker
personas.description: A user seeking mental health support through the app but who is highly sensitive to cost and becomes frustrated when essential features are locked behind paywalls after initial engagement.
goals: Access meaningful mental health insights without needing to pay immediately
Track mood and emotional patterns over time
Receive actionable feedback or reports based on their input
Use the app as a substitute or supplement to therapy
Engage with the app consistently without interruption
Explore features before deciding whether to pay
Feel that their time investment in answering questions leads to value
pain_points: Core features such as reports and insights are locked behind a paywall
Users only discover pricing after investing time in the app
Subscription costs are perceived as too high relative to value
Free version becomes useless after trial period ends
Feeling misled or tricked into engaging before encountering payment
Limited access prevents meaningful mental health support
Frustration when vulnerable users are required to pay for help
Perception that the app exploits mental health struggles for profit
context: Using the app during periods of emotional distress or mental health struggles
Often turning to the app as an affordable alternative to therapy
May be students, unemployed, or financially constrained individuals
Engaging with the app daily (e.g., answering questions over multiple days)
Expecting gradual insights or results after consistent use
Likely to abandon the app once payment is required
May already feel vulnerable or frustrated before encountering paywalls
constraints: Essential features (e.g., reports, insights) must be accessible without mandatory payment
Pricing must be clearly communicated before significant user investment
Free users should still receive meaningful value
The system must avoid delaying paywall exposure after long usage
Subscription pricing should be proportional to perceived benefit
Users should not lose access to previously generated data or insights
The app should not require payment at moments of peak user vulnerability
evidence_reviews: The G1 reviews, same with following personas, just entering review ID as per the template

personas.name: Reliability-Dependent Daily User
personas.description: A user who depends on the app for ongoing mental health tracking and becomes highly frustrated when bugs, crashes, blank screens, login failures, or connection errors interrupt usage.
goals: Open the app reliably whenever needed
Log responses and mood entries without interruption
Access previously saved data and progress history
Use premium and non-premium features without technical failures
Maintain a consistent mental health tracking routine
Trust that app updates will improve, not break, functionality
Continue long-term use without fear of sudden app failure
pain_points: Blank screens prevent answering questions
The app stops working after updates
Login failures block access to personal records
False internet or connection errors appear despite working service
Users cannot access content or navigate tabs
Technical issues disrupt established daily routines
Reinstalling the app does not always solve the issue
Customer support may be slow or unhelpful
context: Using the app repeatedly over weeks, months, or years
Opening the app in response to daily prompts or personal need
Relying on the app during emotionally difficult periods
Already having entered significant personal data and history
Possibly paying for premium features and expecting stability
Experiencing issues after updates, reinstallations, or login attempts
Feeling disappointed because the app was previously useful
constraints: Core app flows must remain functional after updates
Users must be able to log in consistently
Saved data and history must remain accessible
Questionnaires and insights must load reliably
Errors must be accurate and not falsely blame connectivity
Bug fixes should be fast for high-impact failures
The system must not force users to reinstall to regain basic access

personas.name: Privacy-Conscious Sensitive-Data User
personas.description: A user who is willing to use the app for mental health support only if personal information, account access, and historical data are handled securely, transparently, and with user control.
goals: Use the app without unnecessary tracking or third-party sharing
Understand how personal and health information is handled
Retain control over whether an account is required
Keep journal entries and historical data safe over time
Recover or preserve data across reinstalls or device changes
Trust the app with sensitive emotional and mental health information
Use essential features without feeling coerced into data-sharing agreements
pain_points: Third-party sharing of device or personal data
Mandatory account creation for basic use
Requests for too much personal information too early
Lack of anonymous or offline usage options
Loss of past data, exported data, or lifetime access
Account creation failures that block saving progress
App lock or security-related features not behaving reliably
Feeling that privacy tradeoffs are hidden or unfair
context: Using the app for highly sensitive mental health reflection
Entering personal emotional history, symptoms, and journal data
Possibly sharing app results with therapists or doctors
Using the app over long periods and accumulating important history
Changing devices, reinstalling, or trying to restore saved progress
Evaluating whether the app is trustworthy enough to keep using
Being especially cautious because mental health data is more sensitive than normal app data
constraints: Sensitive data handling must be transparent and easy to understand
Users should not be forced into unnecessary data-sharing to access core features
Account creation must be reliable if required
Users should be able to preserve or restore their own data
Security features such as app lock must work consistently
Historical records must not disappear unexpectedly
Privacy choices must be surfaced before major personal disclosure

personas.name: Reminder-Dependent Routine Builder
personas.description: A user who relies on reminders and notifications to maintain a consistent check-in habit and whose engagement drops sharply when notifications are mistimed, excessive, missing, or unreliable.
goals: Receive reminders at the right times to complete check-ins
Build a stable mood-tracking routine over days and weeks
Avoid missing questionnaires due to weak notification support
Use reminders as a prompt during difficult periods
Stay engaged without being overwhelmed by notification volume
Trust that the app will continue reminders consistently
Get enough prompts to complete evaluations and reports
pain_points: Notifications stop appearing altogether
Reminder timing is inconvenient or easy to miss
Too many notifications become intrusive or annoying
Too few reminders make habit formation difficult
Missing reminders causes missed questionnaires and reduced data quality
Notification failures create frustration and anxiety
The app stops prompting after missed entries when the user needs support most
Users cannot rely on reminders to sustain mental health routines
context: Using the app as part of a daily or multi-times-per-day routine
Depending on prompts because manual check-ins are easy to forget
Trying to build consistency during stressful or low-motivation periods
Using the app over extended periods and expecting reminder stability
Needing enough answered questions to obtain evaluations or insights
Balancing mental health support with busy daily schedules
Sometimes wanting customizable intensity rather than one fixed reminder pattern
constraints: Reminder delivery must be reliable over long-term use
Users should be able to receive prompts frequently enough to stay engaged
Notification timing should be appropriate and configurable
The app should not silently reduce reminder support after missed entries
Users must be able to complete required check-ins without reminder failures
Reminder behavior should support both busy and vulnerable users
Notifications must not be so excessive that they drive disengagement

personas.name: Questionnaire-Fatigued User Seeking Relevant Insights
personas.description: A user who wants meaningful reflection and mental health support but becomes disengaged when questions feel repetitive, irrelevant, overly generic, too long, or mismatched to personal circumstances.
goals: Receive questions that feel personally relevant
Gain meaningful insights from repeated app use
Avoid wasting time on repetitive or generic prompts
Use the app to understand specific emotional patterns
Feel that questionnaires reflect real-life circumstances
Complete check-ins without being overwhelmed by text or repetition
See clear value from the effort spent answering questions
pain_points: Questions become repetitive over time
Some prompts do not match the user’s situation or lifestyle
Follow-up questions can feel awkward or ill-fitting
Questionnaires may feel superficial rather than insightful
Too much text makes the experience tiring or disengaging
The app may assume all users are healthy, employed, or similarly situated
Reports are perceived as generic or low-value
Users invest effort without receiving proportionate insight
context: Using the app repeatedly enough to notice recurring question patterns
Seeking more than simple mood logging—wanting actual guidance or understanding
Possibly dealing with disability, stress, shift work, or other nonstandard circumstances
Trying to reflect honestly but feeling the app does not fit their reality
Reading prompts while already mentally tired or emotionally strained
Comparing the app against therapy, other apps, or prior self-help experience
Evaluating whether continued use is worth the effort
constraints: Question sets must remain varied over time
Prompts must be relevant to diverse user situations
The system must avoid assumptions that exclude disabled or nonstandard users
Questionnaire text should be manageable for stressed users
Insights and reports must feel meaningfully connected to user inputs
The app should adapt to user context rather than repeating generic prompts
Users should receive value proportional to the effort of repeated check-ins

personas.name: Engaged Self-Reflection User
personas.description: A user who values the app as a meaningful mental health companion, uses it consistently for reflection and mood tracking, and appreciates clear insights, structured exercises, and supportive daily engagement.
goals: Track mood and mental state consistently over time
Reflect on thoughts and feelings in a structured way
Receive useful insights, exercises, and reports
Use the app alongside therapy or professional care
Build self-awareness and emotional understanding
Feel supported by daily check-ins and app content
Continue using the app long term because it is genuinely helpful
pain_points: A good experience can still be weakened by small feature gaps
Limited emotion options or minor missing features reduce usefulness
Helpful users may still want clearer tracking or richer support
If quality declines over time, highly engaged users lose trust quickly
Even satisfied users may need smoother professional integration
context: Using the app regularly and often enthusiastically
Relying on the app for self-reflection during difficult periods
Sometimes using it with therapist guidance or sharing results externally
Seeing the app as more than a tracker—as a support tool or companion
Responding positively to reminders, exercises, and daily insights
Likely to recommend the app to others when it delivers consistent value
Interested in long-term personal growth, not just short-term symptom logging
constraints: The app must preserve the clarity and usefulness of its existing core experience
Insights, exercises, and reports should remain high-quality and actionable
Mood tracking should stay simple enough for regular use
Features should support long-term engagement without becoming repetitive
The system should remain suitable for use alongside therapy or doctor visits
Helpful support features should be easy to access and understand
The app should maintain user trust through consistent quality
